The prophet was to represent the siege of Jerusalem by signs. He was to lie on his left side for a number of days, which was supposed to be equal to the years from the beginning of idolatry. Everything the prophet presented to the people about the destruction of Jerusalem was to show that sin was the cause of the ruin of that once flourishing city.

The bread that supported Ezekiel was to be made of coarse grain and pulse mixed together, seldom used except in times of urgent scarcity, and he was only to take a small amount. This showed how far the Jews would be reduced during the siege and captivity. Ezekiel does not say, Lord, from my youth I have been raised delicately and never accustomed to anything like this; but that he had been raised conscientiously and never eaten anything forbidden by the law. It will be comforting when we are brought to suffer hardships if our hearts can witness that we have always been careful to avoid even the appearance of evil. See what terrible consequences sin causes, and acknowledge God’s righteousness in this. Because they abused their plenty for luxury and excess, they were justly punished by famine. When people do not serve God with cheerfulness when they have abundance, God will make them serve their enemies when they lack everything.
